summary.intent: Address Codex review findings on PR #665 for the ars-ui repo, specifically fixing selection normalization/desync bugs in Listbox and Select components, then push the fix and shepherd it through CI and Codex re-review.
summary.outcome: Ten Codex review threads were resolved and replied to with commit sha a9cc1889. A follow-up patch normalized selection mode sync (initial invalid values, mode changes via set_props, controlled pruning after item changes) with regression coverage, passing all local checks (fmt, clippy, unit tests, proptest, i18n feature check), and was committed as 'fix(components): normalize selection mode sync'.

repo: Selection state in both Listbox and Select components previously only normalized empty sets, not mode changes or controlled pruning — stale/invalid selections could persist across prop updates.
SyncProps for both Listbox and Select must rebuild ComponentIds from the latest id prop to avoid emitting stale derived ids.
Shift+Arrow key handling requires range-aware ExtendSelection that recomputes the anchor-to-target range, not just appending the next item.
ItemActivated must guard against stale keys and disabled item keys before invoking on_action.
Select trigger Enter/Space should commit the highlighted option while open, not toggle closed.
Hidden select input must not emit 'required'; it should remain only on the accessible trigger.
Open trigger ArrowDown/ArrowUp must dispatch HighlightNext/HighlightPrev to preserve trigger-focused combobox navigation.
Select content_attrs must emit aria-activedescendant for the currently highlighted option.
Controlled pruning must be routed through the controlled mirror so rendered state cannot diverge from stored selection.

code.finding: SyncProps normalization path — needed update to handle mode changes and controlled pruning, not just empty-set normalization.
update_items / invalidate_collection_references region where controlled pruning after item changes needed to be wired through the controlled mirror.
Parallel SyncProps normalization path in Select — same mode-change and controlled-pruning gap as Listbox.
Select update_items region; controlled pruning path needed the same fix as Listbox.

workflow: Used gh GraphQL API to batch-resolve Codex review threads and reply to each with the fixing commit sha in a single shell script.
Poll loop pattern: sleep N seconds then fan out to reactions, gh pr checks, and unresolved thread queries in one command to minimize round-trips.

Ran cargo +nightly fmt --all --check, cargo test -p ars-components selection, proptest with PROPTEST_CASES=256, cargo check with i18n feature, and cargo clippy -D warnings as the local gate before pushing.
Clippy -D warnings must stay clean; two cleanup points in the new normalization helper were caught and fixed before the final commit.

friction: Multiple polling cycles required because GitHub CI jobs (unit tests, clippy, workspace check) complete asynchronously with no push notification — required repeated sleep+query loops.
gh pr checks nonzero exit code on pending jobs cut off the combined poll command early, requiring a separate follow-up query for reactions and unresolved threads.
cargo build lock contention delayed the i18n feature check and clippy runs after tests completed.
Initial fmt check failed after the first patch application, requiring a separate cargo +nightly fmt --all run before re-checking.
Codex continued showing 👀 (watching) state for an extended period with no new threads, making it unclear whether it was still processing or waiting on CI.
open_items: Codex re-review pass after the normalization commit was triggered (@codex review posted) but results were not yet available at session end — any new findings will need a follow-up fix cycle.
Several full CI jobs were still pending at the last recorded check; final green status on all jobs not confirmed in transcript.
Snapshot tests were updated but not explicitly re-run for the normalization patch — only selection-scoped unit/proptest tests were verified locally.
